preamble: Yeísmo is a phonetic shift in Spanish where the palatal lateral [ʎ] (represented by 'll') merges with the palatal glide [j] (represented by 'y'). It is one of the most widespread sound changes in modern Spanish.

examples.note: Most modern Spanish dialects merge /ʎ/ into /j/.

sources: Hualde, J. I. (2005). The Sounds of Spanish.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
